reasoning: The value on 2026-01-18 is 3.5%. The first resolution date is 2026-01-25. The first FOMC meeting in 2026 concludes on January 28. No scheduled rate adjustments will occur before January 25, meaning an increase by the first resolution date is essentially impossible, barring a highly improbable emergency hike. For the second resolution date, 2026-02-17, this captures the outcome of the Jan 27-28 FOMC meeting. Reports from mid-January 2026 indicate that CME FedWatch participants are pricing in a 95% probability of a rate pause (maintaining 3.5%), with the remainder of the probability allocated to a rate cut due to recent soft jobs data. The market implies a 0% probability of a rate hike. Accounting for tail-end model risks, probabilities are assigned at the bare minimum of the allowable range (0.02 and 0.03).
